title: The coins in my glass jar
summary: Explain a small daily saving habit with a coin jar and how it slowly grows into something useful over time.
topic: A small daily habit of saving coins in a jar
prompt: Write 8–10 short sentences explaining a small habit you do every day that adds up over time (saving coins, taking one photo, learning one word). Describe what you do each day, how often you do it, and what happens after many weeks. Use present simple, adverbs of frequency, and 'and'/'but' to link ideas.
hintStarters: One coin is very small, but…
After a few months, I usually…
sampleText: I keep an old glass jar on a shelf in my kitchen. Every evening, after work, I take the coins out of my pocket. I drop them into the jar one by one. I always do this before I cook dinner, so I never forget. One coin is very small, but the sound in the jar makes me happy. I do not check the money often. The jar slowly grows heavy, and the coins climb higher each week. After a few months, I usually take the jar to the table and count everything. The first time, I had enough for a warm winter coat. The second time, I bought new shoes for my daughter. My habit is very simple and it costs me nothing extra. I only save the coins I do not really need. People sometimes laugh and say a coin is nothing, but small things add up. I am patient, and the jar teaches me that a little, every day, becomes a lot. Now my daughter has her own small jar too.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: take the coins out of my pocket
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: lấy những đồng xu ra khỏi túi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: drop them into the jar one by one
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: thả chúng vào lọ từng cái một
trước khi tôi nấu bữa tối
cái lọ từ từ nặng lên

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the coins climb higher each week
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: những đồng xu cao dần lên mỗi tuần

sampleAnnotations.phrase: enough for a warm winter coat
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đủ cho một chiếc áo khoác mùa đông ấm áp
nó không tốn thêm gì của tôi
những điều nhỏ tích lại thành lớn

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a little, every day, becomes a lot
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một chút mỗi ngày trở thành rất nhiều

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Adverbs of frequency for habits
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Để nói về thói quen, dùng các trạng từ tần suất như 'always', 'never', 'sometimes', 'usually'. Chúng đứng trước động từ chính: 'I always do this', 'I never forget', 'People sometimes laugh'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: To describe a habit, use frequency adverbs like 'always', 'never', 'sometimes', 'usually'. They go before the main verb: 'I always do this', 'I never forget', 'People sometimes laugh'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Linking with 'and' and 'but'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'and' để nối hai ý cùng chiều và 'but' để nối hai ý trái ngược: 'One coin is very small, but the sound makes me happy.'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'and' to join two ideas that agree and 'but' to join two ideas that contrast: 'One coin is very small, but the sound in the jar makes me happy.'

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi giữ một cái lọ thủy tinh cũ trên kệ trong bếp. Mỗi tối, sau giờ làm, tôi lấy những đồng xu ra khỏi túi. Tôi thả chúng vào lọ từng cái một. Tôi luôn làm điều này trước khi nấu bữa tối, nên tôi không bao giờ quên. Một đồng xu thì rất nhỏ, nhưng tiếng kêu trong lọ làm tôi vui. Tôi không kiểm tra tiền thường xuyên. Cái lọ từ từ nặng lên, và những đồng xu cao dần lên mỗi tuần. Sau vài tháng, tôi thường mang cái lọ ra bàn và đếm tất cả. Lần đầu tiên, tôi có đủ cho một chiếc áo khoác mùa đông ấm áp. Lần thứ hai, tôi mua giày mới cho con gái. Thói quen của tôi rất đơn giản và nó không tốn thêm gì cả. Tôi chỉ để dành những đồng xu tôi không thật sự cần. Mọi người đôi khi cười và nói một đồng xu chẳng là gì, nhưng những điều nhỏ tích lại thành lớn. Tôi kiên nhẫn, và cái lọ dạy tôi rằng một chút mỗi ngày trở thành rất nhiều. Bây giờ con gái tôi cũng có cái lọ nhỏ của riêng mình.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best describes your daily action?
options: Every evening, after work, I takes the coins out of my pocket.
Every evening, after work, I taking the coins out of my pocket.
Every evening, after work, I take the coins out of my pocket.
Every evening, after work, the coins out of my pocket I take.
mcQuestions.explanation: With 'I' the present simple verb takes no -s: 'I take'.
mcQuestions.hint: What ending does the verb take with 'I'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses an adverb of frequency correctly?
options: I do always this before I cook dinner.
Always I do this before I cook dinner it.
I do this always before I am cook dinner.
I always do this before I cook dinner.
mcQuestions.explanation: The adverb 'always' goes before the main verb 'do'.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does 'always' usually sit in a sentence?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'but' to show a contrast naturally?
options: One coin is very small, but the sound in the jar makes me happy.
One coin is very small, but the sound in the jar make me happy.
One coin is very small, but makes me happy the sound in the jar.
One coin very small is, but the jar sound happy me makes.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'but' joins two contrasting clauses, and 'makes' agrees with 'the sound'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes the change over time most naturally?
options: The jar slow grows heavy, and the coins climbs higher each week.
The jar slowly grows heavy, and the coins climb higher each week.
The jar slowly grow heavy, and the coins climb higher each week.
The jar grows slowly heavy, and higher the coins climb each week.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'grows' agrees with 'the jar', 'slowly' is the correct adverb, and 'coins climb' is correct.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the adverb and both verb endings.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple: 'I ___ this before I cook dinner.'
options: does
doing
do
did
mcQuestions.explanation: With 'I' use the base form 'do' in the present simple.
mcQuestions.hint: It is a habit happening now, with 'I'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence reports a past result correctly?
options: The first time, I have enough for a warm winter coat.
The first time, I had enough for a warm winter coat already past.
The first time, I had enough for a warm winter coat in the future soon.
The first time, I had enough for a warm winter coat.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'had' is the past simple of 'have', correct for a finished event.
mcQuestions.hint: It is a finished moment in the past.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best states the main idea of the habit?
options: A little, every day, becomes a lot.
A little, every day, become a lot.
A little, every day, is becomes a lot.
A little, every day, the becoming is a lot of.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'becomes' agrees with the singular subject 'a little'.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb ending after the subject.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence says you do not check the money often?
options: I do not checks the money often.
I do not check the money often.
I not check the money often.
I do not check often the money very.
mcQuestions.explanation: Negative present simple: 'do not' + base verb 'check'.
mcQuestions.hint: How do you form a negative in the present simple?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence explains the cost most naturally?
options: My habit is very simple and it cost me nothing extra.
My habit very simple is and it costs me nothing extra.
My habit is very simple and it costs me nothing extra.
My habit is very simple and nothing extra it me costs.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'costs' agrees with 'it', and the sentence reads naturally.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence shows other people's reaction with a contrast?
options: People sometimes laughs and say a coin is nothing, but small things add up.
People laugh sometimes and says a coin is nothing, but small things adds up.
People sometimes laugh and say a coin is nothing, but small things adds up it.
People sometimes laugh and say a coin is nothing, but small things add up.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'People' is plural, so 'laugh', 'say', and 'add' take no -s; 'but' shows the contrast.
mcQuestions.hint: 'People' is plural — what verb endings does it need?

criticalThinkingQuestion: What is one tiny thing you could do every single day that would add up to something big after a year, and what stops you from starting it today?
